logs.title: MBR (1541 lines) and EBR (1007 lines) ingested — OMP-2024-0318 loaded
reasoning: MBR reference: MPR-OMP-20-v06 — 1541 lines parsed, 26 sections covering BOM, CPPs, hold times, QC specs, 72-point checklist
EBR batch: OMP-2024-0318 — 1007 lines parsed, covering 6 manufacturing steps across 4 days (2024-03-18 to 2024-03-21)
Product: Omeprazole 20mg Delayed-Release Capsules — hard gelatin capsule with enteric-coated pellets
Manufacturing date range: Day 1 (2024-03-18) through QA disposition (2024-03-27)
Both documents structured and ready for field-by-field comparison across all review categories

logs.title: 72-point checklist complete — 70 pass, 2 fail (hold time H-02 breach + false verification entry)
reasoning: Category 1-3 (Header/BOM/Equipment): PASS — MBR v06 confirmed, all 11 material lots verified, AR numbers present, dual-signed API dispensing, equipment calibration current
Category 4-6 (Process CPPs, IPC, Environment): PASS — all 54 drug-layer and 46 enteric-coat CPP readings within range; all CWG samples pass; acid resistance tests pass; EM logs clean
Category 7-8 (Yield/Reconciliation, QC): PASS — all stage yields above minimums; one OOS (DEV-OMP-2024-0318-001) raised for Stage 1 dissolution, Stage 2 passed, deviation properly closed
Category 9-10 (Signatures, Deviations): FAIL on checklist item 43 — H-02 hold time marked Y when elapsed time was 7h 30m vs 4h max
Total: 70 pass / 2 fail — both failures relate to the same H-02 hold time event

logs.title: CRITICAL finding confirmed: H-02 hold time exceeded 3h 30m — Confidence: 62% — HOLD
reasoning: CRITICAL — Hold Time H-02 Breach: Step 2 ended 23:45 (2024-03-18); Step 3 started 07:15 (2024-03-19); elapsed 7h 30m vs MBR maximum 4h. ANDA-filed constraint — any exceedance requires deviation and QA disposition before progression

CRITICAL (compounding) — False Record in Checklist Item 43: H-02 hold time verification table marks Within Limit as Y, and checklist item 43 is marked Y. Both factually incorrect. Operator V.K. and supervisor T.R. signed off on a materially false entry
No deviation report was raised for the H-02 breach. MBR Section 9.3 requires QA review prior to batch progression
MINOR — CPP log row at 21:15 shows Fluid Air column contains operator name V. Kumar instead of numeric value (m3/h) — ALCOA+ documentation defect
Confidence: 100 - 15 (critical) - 3 (minor) - 20 (ANDA-filed modifier) = 62% — HOLD tier

logs.title: QA escalation drafted — awaiting send approval
reasoning: H-02 inter-step hold time exceeded by 3h 30m (7h 30m elapsed vs 4h maximum)
False entry in checklist item 43 — operator and supervisor signed off incorrect Y
MBR Section 25 (ANDA regulatory note): batches exceeding H-02 must not be released without agency consultation
No deviation report raised — required before any further batch progression
Draft escalation prepared for QA Head to initiate formal deviation and stability impact assessment

artifacts.label: QA Escalation — Hold Time Violation
artifacts.subject: HOLD: Critical H-02 Hold Time Breach — Batch OMP-2024-0318 Omeprazole 20mg
artifacts.body:
Dear QA Head,

Batch OMP-2024-0318 (Omeprazole 20mg Delayed-Release Capsules) has been placed on HOLD following automated batch record review.

CRITICAL FINDING — H-02 Inter-Step Hold Time Exceeded:
Elapsed time between Step 2 and Step 3: 7h 30m (maximum per MBR: 4h 00m). Exceedance: +3h 30m.

MBR Section 25 (ANDA regulatory commitment) states this limit is filed with the agency and any exceedance requires: (1) formal deviation report, (2) stability impact assessment, and (3) determination of whether agency notification is required.

Additional: Checklist Item 43 and the hold time verification table were both marked Y (compliant) by Operator V.K. and Supervisor T.R. despite the documented exceedance. A false record correction deviation is also required.

Required actions:
1. Open deviation report for H-02 exceedance
2. Initiate stability impact assessment per SP-OMP-HT-003
3. Correct checklist item 43 and hold time table
4. Determine if agency notification required per ANDA

Batch remains on HOLD pending QA disposition.

Regards,
Pace — Batch Record Review
Lilly Quality Operations
